channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Quake death toll in Myanmar tops 1,600 and expected to rise as rescue work slowed by damage and war
item.description: Emergency rescue teams on Sunday began trickling into the area of Myanmar hardest hit by a massive earthquake that killed more than 1,600 people.
item.dc:creator: David Rising And Grant Peck, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope's willingness to show his frailty provides an example to young and old alike
item.description: Pope Francis’ frailty was on full view as he left Rome’s Gemelli hospital after five weeks battling pneumonia that nearly killed him.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: With federal election looming, Liberal Canadian PM accused of plagiarism while studying at Oxford: report
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is embroiled in controversy after a Canadian news outlet discovered at least 10 instances of possible plagiarism in his 1995 Oxford thesis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syria swears in new transitional government 4 months after Assad's removal
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Syria’s new transitional government was sworn in Saturday nearly four months after the Assad family was removed from power and as the new authorities in Damascus work to bring back stability to the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hamas says it accepts a new Gaza ceasefire proposal but Israel makes a counter-offer
item.description: The Hamas militant group says it has accepted a new Gaza ceasefire proposal from mediators Egypt and Qatar.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy And Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Faye Hall, American detained by Taliban, has been released
item.description: Faye Hall, an American woman who had been detained by the Taliban since February, has been freed, a source with knowledge of the release told Fox News Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump’s denial of Mexico’s Colorado River request sparks concerns over future water negotiations
item.description: The Trump administration’s unprecedented decision to deny a delivery of water to Mexico is raising alarm among experts, who fear it could jeopardize future cross-border negotiations in an increasingly thirsty region.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, The Hill

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll in Burma, Thailand earthquake rises to more than 1,600 victims
item.description: The death toll from the devastating earthquake that rattled Burma and Thailand on Friday has risen sharply to more than 1,650 victims. Nearly 140 people still missing, Burma's ruling military said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of thousands rally in Istanbul in support of imprisoned mayor
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of protesters again congregated in Istanbul Saturday to how their support for the city's imprisoned mayor and demand his release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says military force not off the table for Greenland after Danish FM scolds his administration
item.description: The Danish foreign minister has scolded the Trump administration for its “tone” in criticizing Denmark and Greenland, saying his country is already investing more into Arctic security and remains open to more cooperation with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Philip Crowther, Kirsten Grieshaber And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK bans ninja swords in move to crack down on violent knife crime
item.description: As knife crime continues to plague the U.K., the Labour government is attempting to crack down by adding ninja swords to the list of banned knives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump effect forces Germany to reprioritize defense as nation plays catch-up in military spending
item.description: In his second term as president, Donald Trump is pushing American allies, including Germany, to do more to fund their militaries and pay their fair share to fund defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts has been announced by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government.
item.dc:creator: David Rising And Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Latest: Major earthquakes strike Myanmar and Thailand
item.description: BANGKOK, Thailand (AP) — A 7.7 magnitude earthquake and an aftershock measuring 6.4 rocked Myanmar and neighboring Thailand on Friday, destroying buildings, a bridge and a dam. At least 144 people were killed in Myanmar, the head of the military government said in the televised speech.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand. Hundreds feared dead
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A powerful earthquake rocked Myanmar on Friday, causing extensive damage across a wide swath of one of the world’s poorest countries and prompting officials to warn that the initial death toll — above 140 — was likely to grow in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: Israel has launched an attack on Beirut, marking its first strike on Lebanon's capital since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war last November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli settlers seen on camera assaulting a Palestinian village. Police arrest only Palestinians
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Over a dozen Israeli settlers attacked a Palestinian village in the southern Israeli-occupied West Bank on Friday, beating residents with sticks and rocks, in an incident captured with rare clarity by security cameras.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel launched an attack on the Lebanese capital Beirut on Friday for the first time since a ceasefire ended fighting between Israeli forces and the Hezbollah militant group in November.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel on Friday launched an attack on Lebanon's capital for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war in November.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Canadian company seeks US permission to start deep-sea mining as outcry ensues
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — An abrupt announcement rattled members of a little-known U.N. agency based in Jamaica that has protected international deep-sea waters for more than 30 years. The Metals Company in Vancouver, Canada said late Thursday that it is seeking permission from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Carlo Acutis, the Catholic Church’s new millennial saint
item.description: Carlo Acutis will become the Catholic Church’s first millennial saint at a solemn canonization Mass on April 27 in St. Peter’s Square. But throngs of pilgrims are already flocking to the church in Assisi, Italy, where his body wearing sneakers, jeans and a sweatshirt lies in a shrine.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Myanmar at a glance: Embroiled in civil war, now facing more devastation after powerful earthquake
item.description: Even before a powerful 7.7-magnitude quake hit Myanmar on Friday, more than 3 million people in the country had been displaced, and hundreds of thousands were cut off from vital food and health programs as a result of a destructive four-year civil war that international groups claim has indiscrimina
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocks Thailand, Burma, collapsing buildings and killing more than 1,000
item.description: A massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake has caused buildings to collapse in Thailand and Burma, leaving more than 1,000 dead across both countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says 'old relationship' with US 'is over' amid tension over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Thursday remarked on his country's relationship with the United States amid tensions over trade agreements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New 2-clawed dinosaur discovered in Mongolia
item.description: Although the new species has large front claws, it is likely a herbivore who used them to grab leaves.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: His brothers’ keeper: Liran Berman calls for urgent action to save his brothers, Gali and Ziv, from Hamas
item.description: Gali and Ziv Berman were taken hostage on Oct. 7, 2023, and their brother, Liran, has been fighting for their freedom ever since. He says his brothers were "the light of every room" they walked into.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Human Rights Council blocks watchdog chief from criticizing UN official accused of antisemitism
item.description: The U.N. Human Rights Council blocked Hillel Neuer from finishing his remarks criticizing Special Rapporteur for the Occupied Palestinian Territories Francesca Albanese.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Cruise passengers issued warning while passing through pirate hot spot
item.description: Passengers aboard the "Queen Anne" ship were issued an alarming warning while passing through a pirate hot spot.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director attacked by Israeli settlers, detained by army
item.description: Co-director Basel Adra witnessed the detention and said around two dozen settlers — some masked, some carrying guns, some in Israeli uniform — attacked the village.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill scores in Gaza as Egypt offers new ceasefire proposal
item.description: Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip killed more than 65 Palestinians over the past day, including women and children, the Gaza Health Ministry said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
